01-01.event: Microsoft moves from Albuquerque to Bellevue, Washington, setting the stage for its rise as a software giant.
The Bell System is broken up by the US government, ending AT&T's monopoly on telephone service and ushering in the era of competitive telecoms.
The World Trade Organization (WTO) is established, with major implications for global digital trade and intellectual property law.

01-02.event: The first commercial CD player, Sony's CDP-101, goes on sale in Japan, launching the digital audio revolution.
The world narrowly avoids widespread Y2K computer failures as systems transition to the year 2000 with minimal disruption.

01-03.event: Satoshi Nakamoto mines the Bitcoin genesis block (block #0), launching the world's first cryptocurrency and blockchain network.

01-04.event: NASA's Mars rover Spirit lands successfully on Mars, beginning its mission to explore the Red Planet's geology.
Apple unveils the iPad, creating the modern tablet market and redefining personal computing.

01-05.event: Eris, the most massive dwarf planet in the Solar System, is discovered by a team led by Mike Brown at Caltech.

01-06.event: The Lunar Prospector spacecraft is launched by NASA on a mission to map the Moon's surface composition and search for water ice.

01-07.event: Steve Jobs introduces the iPhone at Macworld, calling it 'an iPod, a phone, and an internet communicator' — a single device that changed the world.

01-08.event: President George W. Bush signs the No Child Left Behind Act into law, mandating standardized testing that would drive edtech innovation.

01-09.event: Steve Jobs officially unveils the original iPhone at the Macworld Conference & Expo in San Francisco.
The first web browser with a graphical user interface, Erwise, is released by a team of Finnish students.

01-10.event: Amazon's Kindle DX is announced with a larger 9.7-inch screen, paving the way for digital textbooks.
The first web server outside of Europe and North America goes live in Japan, marking the internet's true global expansion.

01-11.event: The recording industry launches copyright infringement lawsuits against hundreds of individual file-sharers, changing digital media enforcement.

01-12.event: IBM announces it has developed the most powerful supercomputer for the Japanese government, capable of 85 teraflops.

01-13.event: America Online (AOL) announces it will acquire Netscape Communications for $4.2 billion, a landmark internet merger.

01-14.event: Nokia announces the acquisition of Trolltech, the Norwegian company behind the Qt framework.

01-15.event: Wikipedia is launched by Jimmy Wales and Larry Sanger, becoming the world's largest and most-used reference work.

01-16.event: NASA's Opportunity rover lands on Mars, beginning a mission that would last nearly 15 years.

01-17.event: Steve Jobs unveils the MacBook Air, the world's thinnest laptop, pulled from a manila envelope to demonstrate its slim profile.

01-18.event: Wikipedia goes dark for 24 hours to protest the Stop Online Piracy Act (SOPA), one of the largest online protests in history.

01-19.event: Google announces major privacy policy changes, consolidating 60+ privacy policies into one across all its services.

01-20.event: NASA's Stardust spacecraft successfully collects samples from Comet Wild 2, the first US mission to return comet samples.

01-21.event: The Concorde begins commercial supersonic flights between London and Bahrain, and Paris and Rio de Janeiro.

01-22.event: Apple releases the Macintosh during Super Bowl XVIII with the famous '1984' commercial directed by Ridley Scott.

01-23.event: Java 1.0 is officially released by Sun Microsystems, bringing the promise of 'write once, run anywhere' to software development.

01-24.event: The first Apple Macintosh goes on sale, featuring a graphical user interface and mouse that would define personal computing for decades.

01-25.event: Microsoft releases Windows 3.1 in China, marking a major push into the Chinese PC market.

01-26.event: The Microsoft Xbox 360 is officially unveiled on MTV, launching the seventh generation of video game consoles.

01-27.event: Steve Jobs unveils the iPad, Apple's first tablet, creating an entirely new product category.

01-28.event: Microsoft releases the Xbox gaming console in North America, entering the video game hardware market.
The Space Shuttle Challenger disaster occurs 73 seconds after launch, killing all seven crew members.

01-29.event: Steve Jobs introduces the iPhone at Macworld Expo San Francisco.

01-30.event: Microsoft releases Windows Vista to the general public, alongside the Office 2007 suite.

01-31.event: Microsoft officially releases Windows Vista and Office 2007 to consumers worldwide.
The first major e-book price war erupts as Amazon and Macmillan battle over pricing, reshaping the digital publishing industry.

02-01.event: The Space Shuttle Columbia disintegrates upon re-entry, killing all seven astronauts and grounding the shuttle fleet for over two years.
Facebook (then called 'TheFacebook') is founded by Mark Zuckerberg, Eduardo Saverin, Andrew McCollum, Dustin Moskovitz, and Chris Hughes at Harvard University.

02-02.event: The first camera phone is released in Europe, the Sharp J-SH04, featuring a 0.1-megapixel CMOS sensor.

02-03.event: NASA's Stardust spacecraft returns samples from Comet Wild 2 to Earth, the first cometary sample return mission.

02-04.event: Mark Zuckerberg launches TheFacebook.com from his Harvard dorm room, beginning the social network that would connect billions.
Google announces Google Apps for Your Domain (now Google Workspace), bringing hosted email and productivity tools to businesses.

02-05.event: Byron De La Beckwith is convicted for the 1963 murder of Medgar Evers, a landmark case in digital evidence collection.

02-06.event: Arthur Ashe, tennis legend and human rights activist, dies of AIDS-related pneumonia at age 49.

02-07.event: The Space Shuttle Atlantis launches on mission STS-122, delivering the European Space Agency's Columbus laboratory to the International Space Station.

02-08.event: The US Congress passes the Communications Decency Act, including Section 230 that would become the foundation of internet speech law.

02-09.event: Intel discloses widespread CPU vulnerabilities (Meltdown and Spectre) affecting nearly every processor made in the last 20 years, forcing industry-wide security overhauls.

02-10.event: The first collision between two satellites occurs when Iridium 33 and Kosmos-2251 collide at 11.7 km/s over Siberia, creating thousands of debris fragments.

02-11.event: The Human Genome Project publishes the first draft of the human genome, a landmark achievement in bioinformatics and computing.

02-12.event: Former US President Jimmy Carter arrives in Cuba for a five-day visit, the first US president to visit since 1928.

02-13.event: Facebook announces the 'Like' button, which would become the most recognized social media interaction in the world.

02-14.event: YouTube is founded by Chad Hurley, Steve Chen, and Jawed Karim, creating the world's largest video-sharing platform.

02-15.event: YouTube.com is officially activated, though the first video would not be uploaded until April.

02-16.event: The last Sony CRT television rolls off the production line, marking the end of the cathode-ray tube era.

02-17.event: Kosovo declares independence from Serbia, becoming the youngest country in Europe.

02-18.event: FBI agent Robert Hanssen is arrested for spying for Russia, one of the most damaging espionage cases in US history.

02-19.event: Fidel Castro announces he will resign as President of Cuba after nearly 50 years in power.

02-20.event: John Glenn becomes the first American to orbit Earth aboard Friendship 7, a milestone in the space race.

02-21.event: Microsoft releases Windows Server 2008 and the first beta of Windows 7.

02-22.event: The first web browser with tabbed browsing, InternetWorks, is released by BookLink Technologies.

02-23.event: NASA's Mars Express orbiter confirms the presence of water ice at Mars' south pole.

02-24.event: WhatsApp is founded by Jan Koum and Brian Acton, growing to become the world's most popular messaging app with over 2 billion users.

02-25.event: NASA's MESSENGER spacecraft performs its first flyby of Mercury, capturing images of previously unseen terrain.

02-26.event: Dolly the sheep is cloned, the first mammal successfully cloned from an adult somatic cell.

02-27.event: Windows Vista is officially released to consumers worldwide by Microsoft.

02-28.event: The first version of the Android operating system is developed by Android Inc. before its acquisition by Google.

03-01.event: Uber is founded by Travis Kalanick and Garrett Camp, revolutionizing urban transportation through a smartphone app.

03-02.event: NASA's Spirit rover discovers evidence of past water on Mars at Gusev Crater.

03-03.event: The first commercial website to accept credit card payments launches, marking the birth of e-commerce.

03-04.event: The International Space Station's crew is doubled to six members, enabling more scientific research in space.

03-05.event: The first draft of the human genome is published in Nature, a milestone enabled by massive parallel computing.

03-06.event: NASA's Cassini spacecraft discovers geysers of water ice and vapor erupting from Saturn's moon Enceladus.

03-07.event: The first version of the social networking site Orkut is launched by Google.

03-08.event: NASA's Mars Exploration Rover Spirit is launched, beginning humanity's most ambitious robotic exploration of Mars.

03-09.event: The Space Shuttle Discovery completes its final mission, landing at Kennedy Space Center after 39 flights.

03-10.event: NASA's Mars Reconnaissance Orbiter enters orbit around Mars, beginning its mission to study the planet's climate and geology.

03-11.event: A 9.0 magnitude earthquake and tsunami strike Japan, causing the Fukushima Daiichi nuclear disaster and major tech supply chain disruptions.

03-12.event: Tim Berners-Lee submits his proposal for the World Wide Web while working at CERN, laying the foundation for the modern internet.

03-13.event: The Large Hadron Collider's first beam test is successfully completed at CERN.

03-14.event: Stephen Hawking, the world's most famous cosmologist and physicist, dies at age 76. His work on black holes and relativity reshaped modern physics.

03-15.event: The first Internet domain name, symbolics.com, is registered by Symbolics Computer Corporation.

03-16.event: Gemini 8 performs the first docking of two spacecraft in orbit, a critical step for the Apollo program.

03-17.event: NASA's Spitzer Space Telescope is launched, studying the universe in infrared for over 16 years.

03-18.event: Alexei Leonov performs the first spacewalk, lasting 12 minutes outside the Voskhod 2 spacecraft.

03-19.event: NASA's Mars Exploration Rover Opportunity lands on Mars, discovering evidence that water once flowed on the planet.

03-20.event: Apple releases iPhone OS 3.0, adding copy-paste, MMS, and landscape keyboard to the iPhone.

03-21.event: Jack Dorsey sends the first public tweet: 'just setting up my twttr', launching the social media platform that would change how the world communicates.

03-22.event: The Intel Pentium processor is formally introduced, with 3.1 million transistors and 60MHz clock speed.

03-23.event: Google announces its acquisition of DoubleClick for $3.1 billion, the largest acquisition in Google's history at the time.

03-24.event: The first version of the Linux kernel 2.0 is released, bringing significant performance improvements.

03-25.event: The European Union launches the Galileo satellite navigation system, Europe's answer to GPS.

03-26.event: The Schengen Area begins operations in seven European countries, removing border controls.

03-27.event: The first version of the video game Half-Life 2: Lost Coast is released, showcasing high dynamic range rendering.

03-28.event: NASA's Mars Reconnaissance Orbiter is launched, the most powerful orbiter ever sent to Mars.

03-29.event: The first version of the Chrome OS operating system is demonstrated by Google.

03-30.event: Microsoft releases Windows Vista Service Pack 1 to manufacturing.

03-31.event: The first version of the Skype VoIP service is released, revolutionizing free internet calling.

04-01.event: Apple Computer is founded by Steve Jobs, Steve Wozniak, and Ronald Wayne in Cupertino, California, starting in Jobs' garage.
Google launches Gmail on April Fools' Day with 1GB of free storage — an unheard-of amount at the time that many thought was a joke.

04-02.event: NASA's MER-A (Spirit) rover launches from Cape Canaveral, beginning its journey to Mars.

04-03.event: Martin Cooper of Motorola makes the first handheld mobile phone call, using the prototype DynaTAC 8000X.

04-04.event: Microsoft is founded by Bill Gates and Paul Allen in Albuquerque, New Mexico, with the vision of 'a computer on every desk and in every home.'
Apple releases the first-generation iPad in the United States, defining the tablet computing market.

04-05.event: NVIDIA is founded by Jensen Huang, Chris Malachowsky, and Curtis Priem, later becoming the world's leading GPU and AI chip company.

04-06.event: NASA's Kepler Space Telescope is launched on a mission to discover Earth-sized exoplanets in habitable zones.

04-07.event: The first video is uploaded to YouTube — 'Me at the zoo' by co-founder Jawed Karim, marking the beginning of the video-sharing era.

04-08.event: The first version of the Twitter API is released, allowing developers to build third-party Twitter clients.

04-09.event: Apple releases the original iPad in the US, selling over 300,000 units on launch day.

04-10.event: GitHub is founded by Tom Preston-Werner, Chris Wanstrath, and PJ Hyett, becoming the world's largest host of source code.

04-11.event: Apple launches the iTunes Music Store, selling songs for 99¢ each and reshaping the music industry.
NASA's Phoenix Mars Lander launches on a mission to search for water ice near the Martian north pole.

04-12.event: Yuri Gagarin becomes the first human in space, orbiting Earth aboard Vostok 1 for 108 minutes.
NASA's Space Shuttle Columbia launches on STS-1, the first orbital flight of the Space Shuttle program.

04-13.event: The first video is uploaded to the internet via YouTube by co-founder Jawed Karim.

04-14.event: The first video goes live on YouTube, a 19-second clip titled 'Me at the zoo'.

04-15.event: Instagram is acquired by Facebook for $1 billion in cash and stock, one of the largest social media acquisitions.

04-16.event: NASA's Cassini spacecraft discovers the first evidence of liquid water on Saturn's moon Titan.

04-17.event: Google completes its acquisition of DoubleClick for $3.1 billion, the largest Google acquisition at the time.

04-18.event: The first version of Twitter's retweet feature is tested by users.

04-19.event: Gordon Moore publishes his famous observation that would become 'Moore's Law', predicting transistor density doubles every two years.

04-20.event: NASA's Gravity Probe B is launched to test Einstein's general theory of relativity.

04-21.event: The first successful heart transplant is performed by Dr. Christiaan Barnard in South Africa.

04-22.event: The first version of the Android operating system is released to the public on the HTC Dream (G1).

04-23.event: The very first YouTube video, 'Me at the zoo' by Jawed Karim, is uploaded to the platform.
Spotify is founded by Daniel Ek and Martin Lorentzon, changing how the world consumes music through streaming.

04-24.event: Microsoft acquires aQuantive for $6.3 billion, at the time the largest acquisition in Microsoft's history.

04-25.event: NASA's Phoenix Mars Lander enters the Martian atmosphere, successfully landing near the north pole to search for water ice.

04-26.event: The Chernobyl Nuclear Power Plant disaster occurs, becoming the worst nuclear accident in history.

04-27.event: Apple releases the third-generation iPad in the US.

04-28.event: The first version of the HTTP/1.1 specification is published as RFC 2068.

04-29.event: Yahoo! rejects a $44.6 billion acquisition offer from Microsoft, a pivotal moment in internet company history.

04-30.event: CERN announces that the World Wide Web technology will be available for anyone to use on a royalty-free basis.

05-01.event: The first BASIC program runs on the Dartmouth Time-Sharing System, birth of the language that would launch the PC revolution.
NASA's Kepler Space Telescope launches on a mission to discover exoplanets, eventually finding over 2,600 confirmed worlds.

05-02.event: The first web browser with integrated email, Netscape Messenger, is released as part of Netscape Navigator 3.0.

05-03.event: The first version of the Android operating system, Android 1.0 alpha, is released as a software development kit.

05-04.event: Google is incorporated as a private company by Larry Page and Sergey Brin in Menlo Park, California.
NASA's Spirit rover lands on Mars, beginning its mission of exploration.

05-05.event: Alan Shepard becomes the first American in space aboard Freedom 7, a 15-minute suborbital flight.

05-06.event: SpaceX is founded by Elon Musk with the goal of reducing space transportation costs to enable the colonization of Mars.

05-07.event: Sony (then Tokyo Tsushin Kogyo) is founded in Tokyo, beginning as a small electronics shop that would become a global technology giant.

05-08.event: The first internet-connected Coke machine goes live at Carnegie Mellon University.

05-09.event: The first version of the Firefox 2.0 browser is released by Mozilla.

05-10.event: IBM's Deep Blue supercomputer defeats world chess champion Garry Kasparov in a six-game match, a landmark moment for artificial intelligence.

05-11.event: IBM's Deep Blue defeats Garry Kasparov in the final game of their rematch, winning the match 3.5–2.5.

05-12.event: A magnitude 7.9 earthquake strikes Sichuan, China, killing over 87,000 people and devastating the region.

05-13.event: AMD demonstrates the first x86-based triple-core processor, the Phenom X3.

05-14.event: The 'I Love You' computer virus spreads worldwide, infecting over 50 million computers in one day and causing billions in damage.

05-15.event: Google launches the first version of Google TV, integrating web content with traditional television.

05-16.event: The first version of the Linux kernel 1.0 is officially released by Linus Torvalds after three years of development.

05-17.event: Facebook goes public on NASDAQ under the ticker FB in the largest tech IPO in history at the time.

05-18.event: Facebook begins trading on NASDAQ at $38 per share, valuing the company at $104 billion.

05-19.event: Star Wars Episode III: Revenge of the Sith is released, setting box office records and showcasing the latest in digital filmmaking.

05-20.event: The first version of the iPhone's SDK is announced by Apple, opening iOS to third-party developers.

05-21.event: Apple releases the iPhone's keyboard software development kit.

05-22.event: Facebook acquires Instagram for $1 billion, a pivotal social media consolidation.

05-23.event: The first version of the Android mobile operating system is demonstrated publicly by Google.

05-24.event: NASA's Mars Odyssey orbiter begins its science mapping mission, discovering vast amounts of subsurface water ice.

05-25.event: President John F. Kennedy announces the goal of landing a man on the Moon before the end of the decade, launching the Apollo program.
Star Wars is released, revolutionizing filmmaking with computer-generated imagery and digital sound.

05-26.event: The first version of the Java programming language, Java 1.0, is released to the public by Sun Microsystems.

05-27.event: Microsoft releases the first version of Silverlight, a web application framework for rich internet applications.

05-28.event: The first Bluetooth-enabled mobile phone, the Ericsson T36, is announced.

05-29.event: The first version of the iPhone's web browser, Safari for iPhone, is demonstrated.

05-30.event: NASA's Phoenix Mars Lander confirms the presence of water ice on Mars.

05-31.event: Elon Musk reveals the Tesla Model S, an all-electric luxury sedan that would redefine the electric vehicle industry.

06-01.event: Apple launches the iPhone 3G and the App Store, opening iOS to third-party developers and creating the app economy.

06-02.event: NASA's Mars Exploration Rover MER-A (Spirit) is launched from Cape Canaveral.

06-03.event: Edward White becomes the first American to walk in space during the Gemini 4 mission.

06-04.event: The Tiananmen Square protests are violently suppressed in Beijing, leading to China's internet firewall and censorship systems.

06-05.event: The Apple II computer goes on sale, becoming one of the first highly successful mass-produced microcomputers.

06-06.event: Tetris, the most iconic puzzle game in history, is created by Soviet engineer Alexey Pajitnov.
The first version of the Google Maps API is released, ushering in the era of location-based web applications.

06-07.event: The first version of the Twitter API is publicly released, allowing developers to build applications on top of Twitter.

06-08.event: NASA's Space Shuttle Atlantis launches on its final planned mission, STS-125, to service the Hubble Space Telescope.

06-09.event: The first iPhone goes on sale in the United States, with thousands lining up outside Apple stores worldwide.

06-10.event: Apple launches the iPhone 3G and the App Store, revolutionizing mobile software distribution.

06-11.event: Apple introduces the iPhone 3G at WWDC, priced at $199 with a 3G connection and GPS.

06-12.event: Apple announces it will transition from PowerPC to Intel processors, a seismic shift in Mac architecture.

06-13.event: The first version of the Android operating system is demonstrated at the Google I/O conference.

06-14.event: The first version of the Chrome browser is released by Google as a beta for Windows.

06-15.event: The first version of Twitter's retweet feature is officially launched.

06-16.event: IBM is founded in Endicott, New York as the Computing-Tabulating-Recording Company by Charles Ranlett Flint.
Oracle Corporation is founded by Larry Ellison, Bob Miner, and Ed Oates in Santa Clara, California.
Donald Trump announces his candidacy for US President from Trump Tower, a campaign that would heavily rely on social media.

06-17.event: The first version of the Google Cloud Print service is announced, enabling cloud-based printing.

06-18.event: NASA's Stardust spacecraft launches on a mission to collect samples from a comet.

06-19.event: Tesla delivers the first Model S vehicles to customers, marking the beginning of the electric vehicle revolution.

06-20.event: Apple releases iOS 4, adding multitasking, folders, and FaceTime to the iPhone.

06-21.event: SpaceShipOne completes the first privately funded human spaceflight, reaching an altitude of 100.1 km.

06-22.event: Apple releases the first version of Safari for Windows.

06-23.event: Reddit is founded by Steve Huffman and Alexis Ohanian in Medford, Massachusetts, growing into the 'front page of the internet.'

06-24.event: Steve Jobs introduces the iPhone 4 at WWDC, featuring the Retina display and FaceTime.

06-25.event: Michael Jackson dies at age 50, causing internet traffic to surge to unprecedented levels as the news spreads globally.
The first version of the Windows 8 operating system is released to manufacturing.

06-26.event: The first iPhone goes on sale in the US, with customers lining up for days at Apple stores nationwide.

06-27.event: The first version of the Microsoft .NET Framework 2.0 is released.

06-28.event: Apple releases the iPhone 3G, the second-generation iPhone with 3G connectivity and GPS.

06-29.event: The original iPhone goes on sale in the United States, marking the beginning of the smartphone era.

06-30.event: Apple releases the iPhone 3G in 22 countries, alongside the launch of the App Store with 500 apps.

07-01.event: Tesla Motors is founded by Martin Eberhard and Marc Tarpenning, later joined by Elon Musk as chairman.
The first version of the Android 1.0 SDK is released to developers by Google.

07-02.event: President Lyndon B. Johnson signs the Civil Rights Act of 1964 into law, a landmark in digital equal access.

07-03.event: The first iPhone goes on sale internationally in the UK, Germany, and France.

07-04.event: Physicists at CERN announce the discovery of the Higgs boson particle, confirmed using the Large Hadron Collider.

07-05.event: Amazon is founded by Jeff Bezos in Bellevue, Washington, originally as an online bookstore.

07-06.event: Google announces Google Gears, a browser extension enabling offline access to web applications.

07-07.event: A series of coordinated terrorist attacks strike London's public transport system, leading to widespread surveillance technology adoption.

07-08.event: NASA's Space Shuttle Atlantis launches on its final mission, STS-135, the last flight of the 30-year Space Shuttle program.

07-09.event: Apple launches the iPhone 3G and the App Store in 22 countries, creating the mobile app economy.

07-10.event: NASA's Mars Pathfinder lands on Mars, deploying the Sojourner rover — the first wheeled vehicle on another planet.

07-11.event: Skylab, NASA's first space station, re-enters Earth's atmosphere and disintegrates over the Indian Ocean and Australia.

07-12.event: Microsoft releases Windows Vista Service Pack 1 to beta testers.

07-13.event: NASA's Mars Exploration Rover Opportunity is launched, beginning its journey to the Red Planet.

07-14.event: Mariner 4 makes the first successful flyby of Mars, sending back the first close-up images of another planet.

07-15.event: Twitter is officially launched to the public, co-founded by Jack Dorsey, Biz Stone, and Evan Williams.

07-16.event: The Trinity test detonates the first atomic bomb, ushering in the nuclear age that would shape supercomputing and simulation.
Apollo 11 launches from Kennedy Space Center, carrying Neil Armstrong, Buzz Aldrin, and Michael Collins on the first lunar landing mission.

07-17.event: The first version of the Android operating system is officially announced by Google.

07-18.event: Intel Corporation is founded in Mountain View, California by Gordon Moore, Robert Noyce, and Andy Grove, becoming the world's largest semiconductor company.

07-19.event: Amazon announces that it now sells more e-books than hardcover books, a pivotal moment for digital publishing.

07-20.event: Neil Armstrong becomes the first human to walk on the Moon, uttering the famous words: 'That's one small step for man, one giant leap for mankind.'
NASA's Viking 1 successfully lands on Mars, sending back the first color images from the Martian surface.

07-21.event: Apollo 11 astronauts return safely to Earth, splashing down in the Pacific Ocean after humanity's first Moon landing.
NASA's Space Shuttle Atlantis lands for the final time, ending the 30-year Space Shuttle program with 135 missions flown.

07-22.event: The first version of the Amazon Kindle is released, selling out within six hours.

07-23.event: Comet Hale-Bopp is independently discovered by two amateur astronomers, becoming one of the most observed comets of the 20th century.

07-24.event: China launches its first space station module, Tiangong-1, marking China's entry into human spaceflight.

07-25.event: The first version of the .NET Micro Framework is released by Microsoft for embedded devices.

07-26.event: NASA's Space Shuttle Discovery launches on STS-114, the 'Return to Flight' mission after the Columbia disaster.

07-27.event: The first version of the Apple iPhone is released internationally.

07-28.event: The first version of the Apple iPhone 3G goes on sale.

07-29.event: Amazon acquires Zappos for $1.2 billion in stock, the largest Amazon acquisition at the time.

07-30.event: Apollo 15's Lunar Module 'Falcon' lands on the Moon at Hadley Rille, the fourth crewed lunar landing mission.

07-31.event: The first version of the Android Market (now Google Play) is launched.

08-01.event: Airbnb is founded by Brian Chesky, Joe Gebbia, and Nathan Blecharczyk in San Francisco.

08-02.event: The first version of the AJAX programming technique is coined by Jesse James Garrett.

08-03.event: Radio Shack introduces the TRS-80 Model I personal computer, one of the first mass-produced microcomputers.

08-04.event: NASA's Phoenix Mars Lander is launched on a mission to search for water ice near the Martian north pole.

08-05.event: NASA's Curiosity rover successfully lands on Mars, deploying the largest and most advanced rover ever sent to another planet.

08-06.event: Tim Berners-Lee posts a summary of the World Wide Web project on the alt.hypertext newsgroup, marking the public debut of the web.
NASA's Curiosity rover lands on Mars, using a revolutionary sky-crane landing system to deliver the car-sized rover.

08-07.event: NASA's Curiosity rover transmits its first color images from Gale Crater on Mars, confirming a successful landing.

08-08.event: The Beijing Olympics opening ceremony showcases cutting-edge digital technology, including LED displays and computer-controlled lighting.

08-09.event: Apple becomes the world's most valuable publicly traded company, surpassing $621 billion in market capitalization.

08-10.event: NASA's Magellan spacecraft arrives at Venus, using radar mapping to reveal 98% of the planet's surface.

08-11.event: Hedy Lamarr and George Antheil receive US Patent 2,292,387 for frequency-hopping spread spectrum technology, a precursor to WiFi and Bluetooth.

08-12.event: IBM introduces the IBM Personal Computer (IBM PC), standardizing the PC architecture with an open design that would dominate the industry.

08-13.event: IBM ships the first IBM Personal Computer (Model 5150) to customers, establishing the open architecture standard for the PC industry.

08-14.event: The first version of the Amazon Kindle is announced, revolutionizing book reading.

08-15.event: Microsoft releases Windows 95, selling 7 million copies within the first five weeks.

08-16.event: Artificial intelligence pioneer Arthur Samuel demonstrates his self-learning checkers program on an IBM 704 computer.

08-17.event: The first commercially produced compact disc rolls off the production line at the Philips factory in Langenhagen, Germany.

08-18.event: Google announces its intent to acquire Motorola Mobility for $12.5 billion, a major move into mobile hardware.

08-19.event: The first internet radio broadcast is made by the WXYC radio station at the University of North Carolina.

08-20.event: NASA launches Voyager 2, humanity's longest-running space mission, still transmitting data from interstellar space.

08-21.event: Google releases Google Apps for Your Domain (now Google Workspace), offering business email and productivity tools.

08-22.event: Microsoft releases Internet Explorer 1.0 for Windows 95, beginning the browser wars.

08-23.event: NASA's Mariner 5 spacecraft flies by Venus, returning data that revealed the planet's extreme atmospheric conditions.

08-24.event: Microsoft releases Windows 95, the most hyped operating system launch in history.

08-25.event: Linus Torvalds posts to the comp.os.minix newsgroup announcing the Linux operating system, beginning the open-source revolution.

08-26.event: Google acquires Android Inc. for approximately $50 million, beginning the development of what would become the world's most popular mobile operating system.

08-27.event: Google TV is officially announced, integrating the web with television.

08-28.event: Samsung unveils the Galaxy Note 10.1 tablet at IFA in Berlin, expanding its stylus-equipped product line.

08-29.event: Netflix is founded by Reed Hastings and Marc Randolph in Scotts Valley, California, starting as a DVD-by-mail rental service.

08-30.event: Apple announces that iPhone sales have exceeded 1 million units, achieving the milestone in just 74 days.

08-31.event: Google releases the first stable version of the Chrome browser for Windows, achieving rapid adoption.

09-01.event: Google is founded by Larry Page and Sergey Brin while they were PhD students at Stanford University.

09-02.event: Google releases the first version of the Chrome web browser, now the world's most popular browser.

09-03.event: eBay is founded by Pierre Omidyar in San Jose, California, as an online marketplace where anyone could buy and sell.

09-04.event: Google is officially incorporated by Larry Page and Sergey Brin, operating out of a friend's garage in Menlo Park.

09-05.event: The first version of the Android operating system is officially announced by Google.

09-06.event: The first version of the Android software development kit (SDK) is released to developers.

09-07.event: Google is incorporated as a private company by Larry Page and Sergey Brin in Menlo Park, California.

09-08.event: NASA's Genesis spacecraft returns samples of solar wind to Earth.

09-09.event: Apple announces that the iPhone has sold over 1 million units in its first 74 days on the market.

09-10.event: The Large Hadron Collider is first powered up at CERN, the world's largest and most powerful particle accelerator.

09-11.event: The September 11 attacks kill nearly 3,000 people, leading to massive changes in surveillance, data mining, and security technology worldwide.

09-12.event: Jack Kilby demonstrates the first integrated circuit at Texas Instruments, a breakthrough that would enable the modern computer chip.

09-13.event: IBM unveils the RAMAC 305, the world's first commercial hard disk drive, a refrigerator-sized machine storing 5 million characters.

09-14.event: The Soviet probe Luna 2 becomes the first human-made object to reach the Moon, impacting the lunar surface at Hadley-Apennine.

09-15.event: Lehman Brothers files for bankruptcy, triggering a global financial crisis that would reshape the tech investment landscape.

09-16.event: Snapchat is founded by Evan Spiegel, Bobby Murphy, and Reggie Brown at Stanford University.

09-17.event: The first Space Shuttle prototype, Enterprise, completes its first free atmospheric test flight at Edwards Air Force Base.

09-18.event: Apple releases iOS 8, introducing HealthKit, HomeKit, and third-party keyboard support.

09-19.event: Apple releases the second-generation iPod Touch, featuring a built-in speaker and volume controls.

09-20.event: Apple reaches 100,000 applications in the App Store, just nine months after its launch.

09-21.event: The first version of the Firefox 1.5 browser is released.

09-22.event: Google releases the first version of the Chrome browser for Windows.

09-23.event: NASA's Mars Climate Orbiter is lost due to a navigation error caused by metric-imperial unit confusion.

09-24.event: Microsoft releases Internet Explorer 8 Beta 2, introducing InPrivate browsing and the SmartScreen filter.

09-25.event: Yahoo! announces layoffs of 10% of its workforce.

09-26.event: The App Store reaches 3,000 applications, demonstrating the rapid growth of the mobile app ecosystem.

09-27.event: The Google search engine is officially launched, indexing 25 million pages.

09-28.event: The first version of the Android 1.0 operating system is released to the public.

09-29.event: Google releases the first version of the Chrome browser for Windows, Mac, and Linux.

09-30.event: The first version of the Android Market (now Google Play) is launched with 50 apps.

10-01.event: The first CD player, Sony's CDP-101, goes on sale in Japan.
The first version of the Android operating system is developed by Android Inc.

10-02.event: Apple releases the iPhone 3G with iOS 2.0 software update.

10-03.event: Facebook announces its acquisition of Face.com, a facial recognition startup.

10-04.event: The Soviet Union launches Sputnik 1, the world's first artificial satellite, sparking the Space Race.

10-05.event: Steve Jobs dies at age 56 after a long battle with pancreatic cancer, marking the end of an era for Apple and the tech industry.

10-06.event: Instagram is founded by Kevin Systrom and Mike Krieger, launching as a photo-sharing app with filters.

10-07.event: Netscape Navigator 1.0 is released, enabling widespread adoption of the World Wide Web.

10-08.event: Apple releases Mac OS X 10.1 (Puma), the second major version of its Unix-based operating system.

10-09.event: NASA's Mars Odyssey enters orbit around Mars, eventually becoming the longest-lasting spacecraft orbiting the Red Planet.

10-10.event: The Sputnik crisis deepens as the Soviet satellite continues orbiting, galvanizing the US to invest heavily in science and technology.

10-11.event: Apple introduces the Macintosh IIsi, one of the first Macs to feature built-in networking.

10-12.event: The Euro currency is officially launched electronically, affecting global financial technology systems.

10-13.event: The first version of Android Market (Google Play) is launched alongside the HTC Dream smartphone.

10-14.event: Chuck Yeager becomes the first person to break the sound barrier in the Bell X-1 aircraft, a milestone in aerospace engineering.

10-15.event: NASA's Cassini-Huygens spacecraft launches on a mission to explore Saturn and its moons.

10-16.event: The Disney Brothers Cartoon Studio is founded by Walt and Roy Disney, eventually becoming a technology-driven entertainment giant.

10-17.event: The Loma Prieta earthquake strikes San Francisco, interrupting the World Series and highlighting the need for earthquake-resistant technology.

10-18.event: The first ARPANET connection is made between UCLA and the Stanford Research Institute, laying the foundation for the internet.

10-19.event: China launches Shenzhou 6, its second manned space mission, carrying two astronauts for a five-day orbital flight.

10-20.event: Microsoft releases Windows Media Center Edition 2004, bringing DVR functionality to the PC.

10-21.event: China's Shenzhou 5 returns safely, making China the third country to independently send a human into space.

10-22.event: The first version of the Ubuntu Linux operating system is released by Canonical.

10-23.event: Apple releases the iPod, a portable music player that would revolutionize the music industry.

10-24.event: Apple announces the iMac with aluminum design, marking a major aesthetic shift in desktop computing.

10-25.event: The US invades Grenada in Operation Urgent Fury.

10-26.event: The first web banner ad appears on HotWired, advertising AT&T with a 44% click-through rate.

10-27.event: The first version of the Wikipedia mobile site is launched, making the encyclopedia accessible on mobile devices.

10-28.event: Motorola announces the Droid, the first Android smartphone with a physical QWERTY keyboard, challenging the iPhone.

10-29.event: The first message is sent over ARPANET, the precursor to the modern internet, from UCLA to Stanford.

10-30.event: Oracle Corporation's precursor, Software Development Laboratories, is founded by Larry Ellison, Bob Miner, and Ed Oates.

10-31.event: The Y2K bug fails to materialize as millions of computers successfully transition to the year 2000.

11-01.event: The first version of the Mosaic web browser (version 1.0) is released, bringing the World Wide Web to mainstream users.

11-02.event: The Morris Worm, one of the first computer worms, infects approximately 10% of all connected computers on the internet.

11-03.event: Facebook opens registration to all users aged 13 and older, expanding beyond college students.

11-04.event: Barack Obama is elected the 44th President of the United States, running the most digitally sophisticated campaign in history.

11-05.event: The first Android-powered smartphone, the HTC Dream (T-Mobile G1), is officially released to consumers.

11-06.event: Barack Obama is re-elected as President of the United States, with social media playing a central role in campaign strategy.

11-07.event: The first version of the Nokia 9000 Communicator is released, pioneering the smartphone category.

11-08.event: Microsoft releases Windows Vista to manufacturing, the successor to the widely used Windows XP.

11-09.event: The Berlin Wall falls, leading to the rapid spread of Western internet infrastructure across Eastern Europe.

11-10.event: Google releases the first version of the Chrome browser for Mac OS X and Linux, expanding cross-platform support.

11-11.event: NASA's Mars Odyssey enters orbit around Mars, beginning its mission to map the planet's surface composition.

11-12.event: The first version of the Windows 8 operating system is released to manufacturing.

11-13.event: The Human Genome Project completes its mapping of the human genome, a $3 billion, 13-year international effort.

11-14.event: Space Shuttle Endeavour launches on STS-126, delivering equipment to the International Space Station.

11-15.event: Sony releases the PlayStation Portable (PSP) in Japan, pioneering handheld gaming with console-quality graphics.

11-16.event: The first version of the Android 1.1 operating system is released.

11-17.event: The first version of the World Wide Web software is released to the public by CERN.

11-18.event: The first version of the Firefox browser (Phoenix 0.1) is released, beginning the challenge to Internet Explorer's dominance.

11-19.event: Pelé scores his 1,000th goal, a moment broadcast live on Brazilian television via satellite.

11-20.event: The first module of the International Space Station, Zarya, is launched into orbit.

11-21.event: Amazon releases the Kindle 2 e-reader, featuring faster page turns and longer battery life.

11-22.event: Toy Story is released, becoming the first entirely computer-animated feature film, produced by Pixar and Disney.

11-23.event: Sony releases the PlayStation 3 in Japan, featuring the Cell processor and Blu-ray disc support.

11-24.event: D.B. Cooper hijacks a Boeing 727 and parachutes with $200,000, in one of the greatest mysteries in aviation history.

11-25.event: Apple releases iPhone Software Update 1.1.2, adding international keyboard support and bug fixes.

11-26.event: The first version of the Android Market (Google Play) is announced by Google, enabling app distribution for Android.

11-27.event: Microsoft releases Windows XP Media Center Edition 2005, integrating TV, music, and photo management.

11-28.event: Mozilla Firefox 1.5 is released, featuring faster page rendering and improved tabbed browsing.

11-29.event: The first iPhone goes on sale in the UK on O2, expanding Apple's smartphone to international markets.

11-30.event: The iPhone launches in Germany on T-Mobile and in France on Orange, completing its initial international rollout.

12-01.event: The first version of the World Wide Web software is released to the public by Tim Berners-Lee at CERN.

12-02.event: The Mars probe Mariner 9 becomes the first spacecraft to orbit another planet, arriving at Mars.

12-03.event: The first SMS text message is sent in the UK, reading 'Merry Christmas' from Neil Papworth to Richard Jarvis.

12-04.event: NASA's Mars Pathfinder is launched on a mission to deploy the first rover on Mars.

12-05.event: Mozilla releases Firefox 1.0, the browser that would break Internet Explorer's monopoly and reshape the web.

12-06.event: NASA's STS-116 Space Shuttle mission launches to rewire the International Space Station's power system.

12-07.event: The attack on Pearl Harbor accelerates the development of codebreaking computers like Colossus and the Bombe.

12-08.event: The first web server in North America goes live at SLAC, extending the World Wide Web beyond Europe.

12-09.event: Douglas Engelbart demonstrates the 'Mother of All Demos,' showcasing the mouse, hypertext, video conferencing, and collaborative editing.

12-10.event: Cisco Systems is founded by Leonard Bosack and Sandy Lerner at Stanford University.

12-11.event: OpenAI is founded by Elon Musk, Sam Altman, Greg Brockman, Ilya Sutskever, John Schulman, and Wojciech Zaremba as a non-profit AI research company.

12-12.event: Microsoft releases the first version of the Xbox Live online gaming service, pioneering console multiplayer gaming.

12-13.event: The Human Genome Project publishes the first comprehensive draft of the human genome.

12-14.event: Netscape Navigator 1.0 is released, becoming the dominant web browser of the mid-1990s.

12-15.event: Microsoft releases the first Xbox gaming console in North America, entering the console hardware market.

12-16.event: The first version of the Quake III Arena game engine is released, showcasing advanced 3D graphics technology.

12-17.event: SpaceShipOne makes its first powered flight, becoming the first privately built spacecraft to exceed Mach 1.

12-18.event: The first communications satellite, Score (Signal Communications by Orbiting Relay Equipment), is launched by the US Air Force.

12-19.event: Sony releases the first PlayStation console in the US, bringing Japanese gaming to a global audience.

12-20.event: Windows 95 OSR2 is released, introducing the Internet Explorer browser and FAT32 file system.

12-21.event: Apollo 8 launches, becoming the first crewed spacecraft to orbit the Moon and the first to witness an Earthrise.

12-22.event: Tim Berners-Lee's World Wide Web software is released to the public, enabling the modern internet.

12-23.event: The transistor is first demonstrated at Bell Labs by John Bardeen, Walter Brattain, and William Shockley, birthing the semiconductor age.

12-24.event: Apollo 8 orbits the Moon, and astronaut William Anders captures the iconic 'Earthrise' photograph.

12-25.event: The Soviet Union is officially dissolved, ending the Cold War and opening up the internet to former Eastern Bloc countries.

12-26.event: A massive 9.1-magnitude earthquake in the Indian Ocean triggers a devastating tsunami, killing over 230,000 people across 14 countries.

12-27.event: The Indian Ocean tsunami devastates coastal communities, spurring the development of early warning systems and satellite-based disaster response.

12-28.event: Sony Pictures is hacked by the Guardians of Peace, exposing sensitive data and raising cybersecurity concerns.

12-29.event: Microsoft invests $150 million in Apple Computer, saving the company from bankruptcy and ensuring continued competition in the tech industry.

12-30.event: Saddam Hussein is executed by hanging, marking the end of a regime whose reign saw the destruction of Iraq's tech infrastructure.

12-31.event: The International Space Station completes its 15th year of continuous human habitation.
